## Page 4

Parameters
Content-Type:
Type: string
Example: application/json
Required: true
Description: The content type. Valid value (application/json).
Client-Request-Id:
Type: string
Example: string
Required: true
Description: A client-generated ID for request tracking and signature creation, unique per 
request. This is also used for idempotency control. Recommended 128-bit UUID format.
Api-Key:
Type: string
Example: string
Required: true
Description: API key provided to the merchant associating the requests with the appropriate 
app in the Developer Portal.
Timestamp:
Type: integer
Example: 0
Required: true
Description: Epoch timestamp in milliseconds in the request from a client system. Used for 
authorization header generation and time limit (5 minutes).
Accept-Language:
Type: string
Example: en-US
Required: false
Description: The Accept Language header contains information about the language preference 
of a user. This HTTP header is useful to multilingual sites for deciding the best language to

nce 
of a user. This HTTP header is useful to multilingual sites for deciding the best language to 

serve to the client. For example en-US or fr-CA.

Type: string

Required: true
Description: Indicates authorization type HMAC, JWT or AccessToken.
Authorization:
Type: string
Example: string
Required: true
Description: Authorization header is used to authenticate the request.

chunks.content:
## STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE

The Terminal/Host message structure consists of three general parts; header, frame data, and CRC. The header and CRC define the frame data and are used for message sending and integrity.

|     | **Header** |    |                     |                |     | **Application Data** |            |               | **CRC** |
|-----|------------|----| --------------------|----------------|-----|----------------------|------------|---------------|---------|
|     | ADR        | CB | **TPDU**            |                |     | MsgType              | Bit Map    | Data Elements |         |
|     |            |    | ID                  | Destination Address | Origin Address |     |            |               |         |
| Bytes | 1        | 1  | 1                   | 2                   | 2              | 2   | 8          | 0-230         | 2       |
| Total | 1        | 1  | 5 (total)           |                     |                |     | variable   |               | 2       |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE

chunks.content: al)           |                     |                |     | variable   |               | 2       |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE

chunks.content:
### Header

The header contains addressing information and message information.

- **ADR** - HDLC (SDLC) Poll address
- **CB** - Message length
- **TPDU** - Transport Protocol Data Unit

|     | **Header** |    |                     |                |     | **Application Data** |            |               | **CRC** |
|-----|------------|----| --------------------|----------------|-----|----------------------|------------|---------------|---------|
|     | ADR        | CB | **TPDU**            |                |     | MsgType              | Bit Map    | Data Elements |         |
|     |            |    | ID                  | Destination Address | Origin Address |     |            |               |         |
| Bytes | 1        | 1  | 1                   | 2                   | 2              | 2   | 8          | 0-230         | 2       |
| Total | 1        | 1  | 5 (total)           |                     |                |     | variable   |               | 2       |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE > Header

chunks.content:
### Transport Protocol Data Unit (TPDU)

The TPDU contains the information related to the destination address and the source address of the transaction.

|     | **Header** |    |                     |                |     | **Application Data** |            |               | **CRC** |
|-----|------------|----| --------------------|----------------|-----|----------------------|------------|---------------|---------|
|     | ADR        | CB | **TPDU**            |                |     | MsgType              | Bit Map    | Data Elements |         |
|     |            |    | ID                  | Destination Address | Origin Address |     |            |               |         |
| Bytes | 1        | 1  | 1                   | 2                   | 2              | 2   | 8          | 0-230         | 2       |
| Total | 1        | 1  | 5 (total)           |                     |                |     | variable   |               | 2       |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE > Transport Protocol Data Unit (TPDU)

chunks.content:
#### TPDU Application

- **TPDU ID** - Identifies the type of TPDU 60h for transactions
- **Destination Address** - International Network Identifier
- **Address Origin** - Identifies the terminal or process that originates the transaction

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE > Transport Protocol Data Unit (TPDU) > TPDU Application

chunks.content:
#### Response TPDU

- **TPDU ID** - Same value as in the request message
- **Destination Address** - Same value as the source address of the request message
- **Source Address** - Same value of the destination address of the request message

---

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > STRUCTURE OF THE MESSAGE > Transport Protocol Data Unit (TPDU) > Response TPDU

chunks.content:
## Applying the Data Structure

Each application message consists of three components in the following sequence; Message Type Identifier, bitmap, and a varying number of data elements. The maximum data content of a message is 240 bytes.

|     | **Header** |    |                     |                |     | **Application Data** |            |               | **CRC** |
|-----|------------|----| --------------------|----------------|-----|----------------------|------------|---------------|---------|
|     | ADR        | CB | **TPDU**            |                |     | MsgType              | Bit Map    | Data Elements |         |
|     |            |    | ID                  | Destination Address | Origin Address |     |            |               |         |
| Bytes | 1        | 1  | 1                   | 2                   | 2              | 2   | 8          | 0-230         | 2       |
| Total | 1        | 1  | 5 (total)           |                     |                |     | 10 - 240   |               | 2       |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > Applying the Data Structure
ISO-8583 Message Specification > Applying the Data Structure

chunks.content:
### Message Type ID

The message type ID consists of 4 digits and is used to define the message type of the transaction. The first and second digits identify the type of message, the third and fourth digits define the mode of function and transmission.

| Digits 1 and 2 | Message Class | Digits 3 and 4 | Streaming Mode |
|----------------|---------------|----------------|----------------|
| 01 | Authorization | 00 | Interactive application |
| 02 | Financial | 10 | Interactive response |
| 03 | File Transfer/Update | 20 | Non-interactive notification |
| 04 | Reverse | 30 | Non-interactive notification response |
| 05 | Closing | | |
| 06 | Administrative | | |
| 08 | Network Test | | |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > Applying the Data Structure > Message Type ID

chunks.content:
#### Type of messages used

| Message Type Identifier | Application |
|------------------------|-------------|
| 0100 | Application for Authorization |
| 0110 | Response to an Authorization Request |
| 0200 | Financial transaction request |
| 0210 | Response to request to financial transaction |
| 0220 | Financial transaction notification |
| 0230 | Response to a financial transaction notification |
| 0320 | Batch Upload or Capture Transaction |
| 0330 | Response to batch upload or capture transaction |
| 0400 | Reverse request |
| 0410 | Request response on reverse |
| 0500 | Request for closure |
| 0510 | Response to the request for closure |
| 0800 | Test Transaction Request |
| 0810 | Request response to test transaction |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > Applying the Data Structure > Message Type ID > Type of messages used

chunks.content:
### Map of Bit The ISO-8583 protocol uses bitmap where each data element is assigned a position indicator in a control field or bitmap. The presence of a data element in a specific message is indicated by a '1' in the assigned position, the absence of an element is indicated by a zero '0'. Each transaction includes a bitmap, a bitmap consists of 64 left-numbered bits, starting with bit 1. The first bit of the map represents: | Bit 1 | Bit 2 | Bit 3 | Bit 4 | .... | Bit 64 | |-------|-------|-------|-------|------|--------| | Field 1
The bitmap secondary
it's always '0' | Field 2
Number of
Count primary | Field 3
Code
process | Field 4
Amount of the transaction | | Field 64
Code
authorization of message |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > Applying the Data Structure > Map of Bit

chunks.content:
### Data Elements

The characteristics of the data element - names, formats, attributes, conditional code values - are fully described in the ISO 8583 specification.

The following rules apply to data elements within a message:

- All data elements start at a byte limit
- Fixed-length 'n' fields with an odd length are right-justified to a byte limit, and zero (0) padded to the left, for example, a field of type "n3" will occupy 2 bytes, and the most significant nibble of the first byte will be "0"
- All lengths for variable-length fields are represented in binary-coded decimal (BCD), right-justified byte limit, and zero(0) padded left, e.g. an 'LLVAR' field with a length of 15, will have a '15h' indicator occupying one byte. A 'LLLVAR' field with a length of 15 will have a length indicator of '0015h', which takes up 2 bytes
- The length indicator for a variable-length field for a count of the number of data elements to track, that does not include the length of the length indicator
- The variable-length main account number field, field 2, with an odd length is left justified inside the field and filled with "F"

chunks.content: nt number field, field 2, with an odd length is left justified inside the field and filled with "F"

chunks.content:
### Description of Attributes

The attribute column lists the format and size of the data element, the field size is the number of the data elements contained in the field.

Variable-length fields are displayed with the characters "." Indicating the number of variable-length digits (... 999 defines a field that has a maximum length of 999 data elements)

The following table defines each attribute:

| Abbreviation | Description | Size |
|--------------|-------------|------|
| a | Alphabetic characters (a-z, A-Z) | Each data element represents one byte |
| n | Numeric digits (0-9) | Each data element represents 1 nibble (2 data elements = 1 byte) |
| s | Special characters | Each data element represents one byte |
| an | Alphabetic and numeric characters (0-9, a-z, A-Z) | Each data element represents one byte |
| as | Special and alphabetic characters | Each data element represents one byte |
| ns | Numeric and special characters | Each data element represents one byte |
| Ans | Alphabetic, numeric, and special characters | Each data element represents one byte |

| ss | Second | |
| LL,LLL | Variable field length that follows | |
| VAR | Variable length field | |

| .. 17 | Variable length of a maximum of 17 characters. All variable fields contain two or three positions at the beginning of the field to identify the following positions | |
| b | Binary data | Each data element represents 1 bit. (8 data elements = 1 byte) |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FORMAT > Description of Attributes

chunks.content:
### Transaction Response and Submission Codes In the transaction tables, a code will be included in the field of the sending and response that will indicate whether the data should be included according to the transaction. The codes are defined below: | Code | Description | |------|-------------| | M | Mandatory | | O | Optional | | C xx | Conditional field where xx is:
**01** - Account number is included when the transaction is made manually via keypad.
**02** - The expiration date is included when the transaction is made manually via keyboard.
**03** - For online transactions when the card number is read from a magnetic card reader. track I and/or track II must be included.
**04** - The system stores only the primary account number and expiration date of a track read. Any transaction processed with the exception of an online transaction shall include the Primary account number and expiration date.
**05** - The first two digits of the position entry mode will be set to '02' if the card is read by the card reader, and '01' if the card number is entered via the keypad. The digit past will indicate if there is PIN reading capacity, the value will be 1 if it has or 2 if it does not have the capacity, see 'POS Entry Mode' table.
**06** - Since the tracks cannot be stored in a transaction, the card number must be taken and Expiration date for batch cancellation or upload in a closure.
**07** - Transaction made from a terminal with ICC EMV capabilities. |

chunks.content:
## TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS

This section describes the implemented ISO-8583 fields.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS

chunks.content:
### (DE-2) Main Account Number

The primary account number (PAN) contains the card number when the transaction is executed manually. The terminal does not store the track information in the batch, therefore the PAN is sent in all advice transactions such as adjustment, reverse, etc.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-2) Main Account Number

chunks.content:
### (DE-3) Code Process

The process code is used in conjunction with the message type to define the type of transaction that is being sent from the terminal to the host, and account selection information is also included.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-3) Code Process

chunks.content:
#### Account Selection

Account information is represented in the third and fourth digits of the process code. See the table below.

| Code | Selected Account |
|------|------------------|
| 00 | Default account |
| 10 | Savings account |
| 20 | Current account |

If the account selection option is active on the card, the cardholder is prompted to select the account on the terminal or pinpad screen and included in item 3 of the process code.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-3) Code Process > Account Selection

chunks.content:
#### Definition of Processing/Flow Control

In the host's responses to the terminal, the host typically uses the same process code that the terminal sends to it in the request, however, it is intended that the host will send additional information to the terminal using the last digit bit of the process code, the host must set one of these bits at a time.

| Bit | Definition of Bit | Description |
|-----|-------------------|-------------|
| 2 | Initialize after the transaction | It tells the terminal to perform the initialization process after finishing the transaction in process, This bit can be used by the host to force the terminal to perform a parameter load |
| 1 | Force lot closure | When this bit is sent, the terminal signals the merchant that the closing process must be executed, the terminal crashes and does not allow another transaction to be made until the closing process is executed |
| 0 | More message indicators | This bit indicates that there are more messages to send, it is normally used in the initialization response to instruct the terminal to send another initialization message and thus allow the host to upload the next data packet to the terminal, it is also used when the terminal performs a batch upload, the terminal uses this bit to instruct the host that there are still transactions to be sent, but in the last packet, this bit must travel in clear |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-3) Code Process > Definition of Processing/Flow Control

chunks.content: at there are still transactions to be sent, but in the last packet, this bit must travel in clear |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-3) Code Process > Definition of Processing/Flow Control

chunks.content:
### (DE-4) Transaction amount

In this field travels the total amount of the transaction, if the terminal supports tip and tax, the base amount of the transaction is calculated by subtracting the amount of the tip and tax.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-4) Transaction amount

chunks.content:
### (DE-11) STAN

The terminal generates this data automatically for each transaction processed, this data is required by the terminal in the response as it is used to validate, it is STAN must be used as a method to identify a transaction, The terminal must not generate a STAN with a value '000000'

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-11) STAN

chunks.content:
### (DE-12) Local Transaction Time

In this field, the time of the original transaction travels, this data does not change in subsequent transactions such as the adjustment transaction.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-12) Local Transaction Time

chunks.content:
### (DE-13) Transaction Date Local

In this field travels the date of the original transaction, this data does not change in subsequent transactions such as the adjustment transaction.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-13) Transaction Date Local

chunks.content:
### (DE-14) Expiration Date

In this field travels the expiration date of the card, it is included when the track data is not present, the terminal does not store the tracks in the batch, therefore, the PAN data and expiration date are sent in advice transactions as settings.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-14) Expiration Date

chunks.content:
### (DE-22) POS Entry Mode

POS input mode indicates how PAN data was captured on the terminal

| Positions 1 and 2 | Input Mode | Position 3 | PIN entry capability |
|-------------------|------------|------------|----------------------|
| 00 | Unspecified | 0 | Unspecified |
| 01 | Manual entry | 1 | PIN capability |
| 02 | Magnetic Stripe | 2 | No PIN capability |
| 05 | Chip EMV | | |
| 07 | Chip by ContacLess | | |
| 80 | FallBack | | |
| 91 | Band by ContacLess | | |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-22) POS Entry Mode

chunks.content:
### (DE-24) NII

The international network identifier is used to identify the acquiring host

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-24) NII

chunks.content:
### (DE-25) POS Condition Code

It is used to identify the condition under which the transaction is carried out.

| Code | Meaning | State |
|------|---------|-------|
| 00 | Normal presentation | |
| 01 | Customer does not show | Not implemented |
| 03 | Suspicious trade | Not implemented |
| 04 | ECR interface | Not implemented |
| 05 | Card not present | |
| 06 | Request for prior authorization | Not implemented |
| 08 | Order by mail and/or phone | Not implemented |
| 51 | Account open | Not implemented |
| 52 | Frequent transaction | Not implemented |
| 71 | Card present with damaged band | Not implemented |
| 87 | Storage and shipping | Not implemented |
| 91 | Proximity payment with magnetic stripe by means of an antenna | Not implemented |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-25) POS Condition Code

chunks.content:
### (DE-35) Track II data

The track II field is present when the magnetic stripe transaction is initiated and the magnetic stripe contains the valid track data, the start and end sentinel data and the LRC characters are excluded in the field. The track is not stored in the batch, the card number and expiration date are extracted from it and it is the one that is saved in the batch.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-35) Track II data

chunks.content:
### (DE-37) RRN – Reference Number

It is a data that comes from the host, the terminal stores the reference number which is included in the notification transactions such as the setting. If a new reference number is received in a notification transaction, the terminal will replace the previous value.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-37) RRN – Reference Number

chunks.content:
### (DE-38) Authorization Number

Value sent by the host when a transaction is approved.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-38) Authorization Number

chunks.content:
### (DE-39) Response Code

Value returned by the host to indicate the status of the transaction, a response code "00" indicates a status of pass, any other value indicates a status of denied or error message.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-39) Response Code

chunks.content:
### (DE-41) Terminal ID

The terminal ID is used only to identify the terminal.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-41) Terminal ID

chunks.content:
### (DE-42) Acquirer ID

This is the merchant number assigned to the terminal.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-42) Acquirer ID

chunks.content:
### (DE-43) Name of Acquirer

In this field travels the name of the business. It is not currently used by BAC Credomatic.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-43) Name of Acquirer

chunks.content:
### (DE-45) Track I data

Track I data is present when magnetic stripe reading is used to initiate a transaction, within the field are data excluding start and end sentries and LRC. The track data is not stored in the batch, the PAN data and expiration date are extracted to be stored and used in second transactions such as cancellation.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-45) Track I data

chunks.content:
### (DE-48) Additional Data

This field is used to send the electronic series of the terminal in the statistics message.

| Field | Attribute | Bytes | Values |
|-------|-----------|-------|--------|
| Length | n 4 | 2 | '0024' – BCD length of data |
| Serial Number | Ans.. 24 | 24 | '9999999    ' right-justified filled with zeros |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-48) Additional Data

chunks.content:
### (DE-52) Personal Identification Number PIN

In this field, the pinblock travels in encrypted form in DUCTP format.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-52) Personal Identification Number PIN

chunks.content:
### (DE-55) Integrated Circuit Board – ICC

The ICC System Related Data field contains the information required by the acquirer to complete an EMV credit or debit transaction supported by an issuer, in this field the chip-specific data travels.

EMV-compatible ICC data has its own generic structure and may contain one or more private data sub-elements (PDS), see attached figure.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-55) Integrated Circuit Board – ICC

chunks.content:
#### Structure of the DE-55

```
0LLL Variable data zone - max 255 bytes
     one or more PDS occurrences
     
Length | First PDS | Second PDS | n PDS

     TLV Encoding
     
PDS Tag (b... 2) | Length (b 1) | PDS Data (b... 127)
```

Each PDS within the DE-55 contains 3 sub-fields:

1. **PDS Tag**: Contains the Tag ID of the EMV data object sent in the frame in hexadecimal format
2. **PDS Length**: Specifies the byte length of PDS data, expressed as binary in the range of 1 to 127
3. **PDS Value**: Contains the value of the corresponding EMV data object, as identified by the Tag ID

PDSs can appear in any order within the DE-55, therefore the entire length of the field should be taken to ensure that all PDSs are captured during message processing.

| Field/PDS | Attribute | Bytes | Values |
|-----------|-----------|-------|--------|
| Length Attribute | n 3 | 2 | 0LLL-BCD Full length of DE-55, Max. 255 bytes |
| Tag '5F2A' | M n 16 | 2 | Currency code set up on the terminal |
| Tag '71' & '72' (Answer) | O b ... 128 | | Issuer script template that comes in the response frame |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-55) Integrated Circuit Board – ICC > Structure of the DE-55

chunks.content:

| Tag '82' | M b 16 | 2 | Application Interchange Profile, specifies the application functions that are supported by the card, the it attempts to execute only those functions that the ICC supports |
| Tag '84' | O b Var .. 16 | | Application Identifier (AID) / Dedicate File Name (DF) |
| Tag '91' (Answer) | O b Var 16 | | Issuer Authentication Data, travels in the response if the issuer's online authentication is required |
| Tag '95' | M b 40 | 5 | Terminal Verification Result (TVR) |
| Tag '9A' | M n 6 | | Transaction Date format 'YYMMDD' |
| Tag '9C' | M n 2 | 1 | Transaction Type – Taken from the transaction data |
| Tag '9F02' | M n 12 | 6 | Transaction amount |
| Tag '9F03' | M n 12 | 6 | Other Amount – Associated with the CashBack transaction |
| Tag '9F09' | O b 16 | 2 | Terminal Application Version Number |
| Tag '9F10' | M b Var .. 32 | | Issuer Application Data (IAD) |
| Tag '9F1A' | M n 3 | 2 | Terminal Country Code |

chunks.content:
### (DE-60) Field 60 Private Data

The following section defines the use of each field.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-60) Field 60 Private Data

chunks.content:
#### Lot Number

The batch number is sent to the host in the closing message (0500), the terminal assigns this number when the batch is closed. Batch numbers can be set in the terminal by means of initialization.

| Field | Attribute | Bytes | Value |
|-------|-----------|-------|-------|
| Attribute Length | n 3 | 2 | '0006' – BCD Data Length |
| Lot Number | Ans 6 | 6 | |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-60) Field 60 Private Data > Lot Number

chunks.content:
#### Original amount

The original amount field is used to send the original transaction data in a reversal or adjustment.

**Message Type (0220)**

| Field | Attribute | Bytes | Value |
|-------|-----------|-------|-------|
| Attribute Length | n 3 | 2 | '0012' – BCD Data Length |
| Original amount | An 12 | 12 | Amount being adjusted, 10 integers 2 decimal places |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-60) Field 60 Private Data > Original amount

chunks.content:
#### Original Data of the Message

It is used in the message type (0320) and travels the message type data of the original transaction as well as the STAN value of the original transaction.

| Field | Attribute | Bytes | Value |
|-------|-----------|-------|-------|
| Attribute Length | n 3 | 2 | '0022' – BCD Length of Data |
| Message Type | an 4 | 4 | Original transaction value |
| STAN | an 6 | 6 | The value of the original transaction's STAN |
| Reserved | an 12 | 12 | Fill Gaps – Reserved for future use |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-60) Field 60 Private Data > Original Data of the Message

chunks.content:
#### Software ID

The POS application ID is sent in the statistics message (0800) after closing.

| Field | Attribute | Bytes | Value |
|-------|-----------|-------|-------|
| Attribute Length | n 3 | 2 | '0035' – BCD Data Length |
| Original amount | an 1 | 1 | 'A... Z' Statistics plot version |
| Software Version | Ans 34 | 34 | |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-60) Field 60 Private Data > Software ID

chunks.content:

chunks.content:
#### CVV2 Data (Tag16) Tag used in the request for a manual transaction, and is used to authenticate the physical presence of the card when the card-holder is not present at the time of the transaction, in the host's response the result may or may not come, this result code does not influence to deny a transaction, that is, if the value of bit 39 is equal to '00' or '08' the transaction must be approved indistinctly of response to this tag. A flag must be in place to activate and deactivate this tag. | Field | Attribute | Bytes | Values | |-------|-----------|-------|--------| | Length of additional data | n 4 | 2 | '0LLL' – BCD length of data | | Tag ID | Ans 2 | 2 | '16' CVV2 Data | | Processing - Shipping Value | an 6 | 6 | The 6 characters of the CVV2 to be sent to the Host

**Position 1**
0 - CVV2 Not intentionally provided
1 - CVV2 Is present
2 - CVV2 It is present on the card but is illegible
9 – The CVV2 is not printed on the card

**Position 2**
0 – Return only the Response Code of the transaction
1 – Return the Response code and the CVV2 validation result

**Positions 3-6**
The value of the CVV2 printed on the card (right-justified filled with spaces) If position 1 = 0, 2 or 9, positions 3-6 must be filled with spaces |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > TRANSACTION DATA FIELDS > (DE-63) Data Private > CVV2 Data (Tag16)

chunks.content:
justified filled with spaces) If position 1 = 0, 2 or 9, positions 3-6 must be filled with spaces | | Response processing - Result Code | an 1 | 1 | The first character of the result returned by the host:
M - CVV2 matches
N - CVV2 Does not match
Q – Not prosecuted
S – It was indicated that CVV2 is not present on the card
U – User is not certified |

chunks.content:
## DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT

This section defines the transactions that are used by the BAC Credomatic POS application.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT

chunks.content:
### Sale

In BAC Credomatic's point-of-sale terminals this transaction is by default, i.e. if the card is swiped, inserted or typed on the main screen, the sales transaction must be initiated.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale

chunks.content:
#### Format definition

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | n 4 | 0200 | 0210 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 2 | Primary Acct. Num. | n .. 19 | C01 | | |
| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | 000000 | 000000 | |
| 4 | Amount, Trans. | n 12 | M | Or | |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | | M | hhmmss |
| 13 | Date, Local Trans. | n 4 | | M | MM/DD |
| 14 | Date, Expiration | n 4 | C02 | | YY/MM |
| 22 | POS Entry Mode | n 3 | C05 | | |
| 24 | NII | N 4 | M | M | |
| 25 | POS Condition Code | n 2 | 00 | | |
| 35 | Track 2 Data | z .. 37 | C03 | | |
| 37 | Retrieval Ref. No. | an 12 | | M | |
| 38 | Auth. Id. Response | an 6 | | M | |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | | M | |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 45 | Track 1 Data | Ans .. 76 | C03 | | |
| 55 | ICC Sys Related Data | b … 255 | M | Or | When the transaction is EMV |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale > Format definition

chunks.content:
ns .. 76 | C03 | | |
| 55 | ICC Sys Related Data | b … 255 | M | Or | When the transaction is EMV |

| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans ... 999 | M | | |
| 63 | Additional Data | Ans … 999 | Or | Or | View Tag16 |

**NOTES:**

Fast payment is a flag at the application level that what you do is change the flow of data capture in a transaction, this in order to speed up the collection by enabling the 4 forms of capture at the same time, in this modality the keyboard is enabled to enter the amount and not the card number at the beginning.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale > Format definition

In the application you must have 3 different ways to configure the additional amounts, which are:

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale with tax and gratuity > Format definition

chunks.content:
#### Normal Settings

By enabling this way of operating, when the entry of the tax and tip amounts is requested on the screen, you can enter in each of them, presenting in the confirmation screen of amounts for both in (0), if this is the case, the DE54 does not travel in the frame to the host.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale with tax and gratuity > Normal Settings

chunks.content:
#### Automatic configuration

In this configuration mode, the terminal takes the amount of the percentage defined in both tax and tip fields and adds it to the base amount without asking for it.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale with tax and gratuity > Automatic configuration

chunks.content:
#### Forced configuration

In this configuration mode the terminal will request the entry of the amount of the tax and tip and unlike the normal configuration, here it is not allowed to press enter without first entering a value, this value can be zero and bit54 and 63 must travel.

**NOTES:**

This transaction is supported in EMV, it can be done by chip and cless.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Sale with tax and gratuity > Forced configuration

chunks.content: ### Adjustment

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Adjustment

chunks.content:
#### Format definition

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | n 4 | 0220 | 0230 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 2 | Primary Acct. Num. | n .. 19 | C01 | | |
| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | 020000 | 020000 | |
| 4 | Amount, Trans. | n 12 | M | Or | |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | |
| 12 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | Or | M | |
| 13 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | Or | M | hhmmss |
| 14 | Date, Expiration | n 4 | C02 | | YYMM |
| 22 | POS Entry Mode | n 3 | C05 | | |
| 24 | NII | n 4 | M | M | |
| 25 | POS Condition Code | n 2 | 00 | | |
| 37 | Retrieval Ref. No. | an 12 | M | M | Same as the original transaction |
| 38 | Auth. Id. Response | an 6 | M | | Same as the original transaction |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | | M | |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 54 | Additional Amounts | an... 120 | Or | Or | See Bit54 |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Adjustment > Format definition

chunks.content:
42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 54 | Additional Amounts | an... 120 | Or | Or | See Bit54 |

| 60 | Private Use | Ans... 999 | | | Amount travels before being adjusted |
| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans... 999 | Or | | |

**NOTES:**
- The amount of tip and tax may be adjusted
- Each of the above amounts must have a separate flag at the application level with which the adjustment is enabled or disabled, in addition to having a separate percentage validation for the adjustment
- A txn may be adjusted a number of times before closing
- In an adjustment transaction, a receipt is not printed, but the sale prior to the adjustment is reprinted, it must print the new data

chunks.content:
### Sell More Cash

Transaction through which in addition to making the sale, a cash withdrawal is made. The withdrawal amount is a percentage value to the base amount and must be configured at the level of the application that manages the terminal profiles.

chunks.content:

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | n 4 | 0200 | 0210 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 2 | Primary Acct. Num. | n .. 19 | C01 | | |
| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | 090000 | 090000 | |
| 4 | Amount, Trans. | n 12 | M | Or | |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | | M | hhmmss |
| 13 | Date, Local Trans. | n 4 | | M | MMDD |
| 14 | Date, Expiration | n 4 | C02 | | YYMM |
| 22 | POS Entry Mode | n 3 | C05 | | |
| 24 | NII | n 4 | M | M | |
| 25 | POS Condition Code | n 2 | 00 | | |
| 35 | Track 2 Data | z .. 37 | C03 | | |
| 37 | Retrieval Ref. No. | an 12 | | M | |
| 38 | Auth. Id. Response | an 6 | | M | |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | | M | |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 45 | Track 1 Data | Ans.. 76 | C03 | Or | |
| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans... 999 | Or | | |

| | |
| 45 | Track 1 Data | Ans.. 76 | C03 | Or | |
| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans... 999 | Or | | |

| 63 | Additional Data | Ans... 999 | M | | See tag41 |

**Notes:**

This transaction is not supported by EMV in the BAC application

chunks.content: ### Discount Sale

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Discount Sale

chunks.content:
#### Format definition

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | n 4 | 0200 | 0210 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 2 | Primary Acct. Num. | n .. 19 | C01 | | |
| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | 000000 | 000000 | |
| 4 | Amount, Trans. | n 12 | M | Or | |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | | M | hhmmss |
| 13 | Date, Local Trans. | n 4 | | M | MM/DD |
| 14 | Date, Expiration | n 4 | C02 | | YY/MM |
| 22 | POS Entry Mode | n 3 | C05 | | |
| 24 | NII | N 4 | M | M | |
| 25 | POS Condition Code | n 2 | 00 | | |
| 35 | Track 2 Data | z .. 37 | C03 | | |
| 37 | Retrieval Ref. No. | an 12 | | M | |
| 38 | Auth. Id. Response | an 6 | | M | |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | | M | |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 45 | Track 1 Data | Ans .. 76 | C03 | | |
| 55 | ICC Sys Related Data | b … 255 | M | Or | When the transaction is EMV |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Discount Sale > Format definition

chunks.content:
ns .. 76 | C03 | | |
| 55 | ICC Sys Related Data | b … 255 | M | Or | When the transaction is EMV |

| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans ... 999 | M | | |
| 63 | Additional Data | Ans … 999 | M | Or | View Tag80 |

**NOTES:**

The discount at the terminal only works for informational purposes, i.e. the terminal will perform the calculation to print it on both receipts (merchant, customer). In bit 4 (Amount Trans.) 100% of the sale travels, the value of the discount only travels in bit63. At closing, the terminal must send the values of bit 4, that is, the amounts without discounts since it is the host who performs the operation. If we take the case of the example transaction, the terminal will have to send the closing by C200.00

The amount of the discount must be parameterizable in the application of the pos and is automatically calculated according to the percentage value.

**NOTES:**
- Fleet tags must be configurable at the application level in order to be renamed
- The submission of the point is mandatory
- In the entry of gallons, the behavior of the field is changed so that it is displayed on the screen 0000,000. The expected behavior is similar to the amount field but with the particularity of the three decimal places, so that, depending on the entry, the numbers move from the decimal side to the next whole. The point must travel in the plot
- Name of the cardholder captured from the card. In case the name of the cardholder of the card cannot be read, the TH line will be printed, showing the field "blank or empty"

| 4 | Amount, Trans. | n 12 | M | Or | |

Ans .. 76 | C03 | | |
| 55 | ICC Sys Related Data | b … 255 | M | Or | If the transaction is EMV |


**NOTES:**

In a check-in, no invoice number is generated, i.e. field 62 does not travel. Receipt printing must be optional for a check-in transaction, the application must have a flag where it can be disabled. The terminal must present the authorization number on the screen and must not change until there is intervention by the person who is handling the equipment if printing is disabled for authorizations.

**NOTES:**
- There must be a menu at the application level where each item of the payment plan can be selected separately "CR", "EX"; 2 ways of entering the terms can be configured at the application level, one by a range between XX and YY, where the terminal requests a value to be entered manually and this is within the minimum and maximum range configured. The second option will be to configure at least 8 different values in the application, the terminal will ask you to select the term that shows previously configured on the screen
- On the terminal screen it must be grouped as follows: if "CR" and "EX" or one of them are activated, it must be presented under the "PAYMENT PLAN" menu

chunks.content:
### Reverse

A reversal occurs due to a communication error and the terminal does not receive a response from the host, with the reversal the terminal tells the host that this transaction will be removed from the batch.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Reverse

chunks.content:
#### Format definition

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | n 4 | 0400 | 0410 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 2 | Primary Acct. Num. | n .. 19 | C01 | | |
| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | M | M | Same as the original transaction |
| 4 | Amount, Trans. | n 12 | M | Or | |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | Same as the original transaction |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | | M | hhmmss |
| 13 | Date, Local Trans. | n 4 | | M | MMDD |
| 14 | Date, Expiration | n 4 | C02 | | YYMM |
| 22 | POS Entry Mode | n 3 | C05 | | |
| 24 | NII | n 3 | M | M | |
| 25 | POS Condition Code | n 2 | M | | |
| 37 | Retrieval Ref. No. | an 12 | | M | |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | | M | The host must always answer '00' |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans... 999 | M | | Same as the original transaction |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Reverse > Format definition

chunks.content:
| Ans 15 | M | | |
| 62 | Invoice/ECR Ref# | Ans... 999 | M | | Same as the original transaction |


**NOTES:**

The reverse must be executed automatically from the client and will be sent when another one is executed transaction, sending the reverse first and after receiving the response, the system must send the next transaction. In the answer (410) there is no field 38, it does not apply to a reverse. This transaction does not print receipt.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Reverse > Format definition

The process by which the host is informed that a previously authorized transaction will be removed from the batch. This must have the application-level option that is done online or offline.

| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | 020000 | 020000 | It is equal to the adjustment |

| 24 | NII | n 3 | M | M | Same as the original transaction |
| 25 | POS Condition Code | n 2 | 00 | | |
| 35 | Track 2 Data | z .. 37 | | | |
| 37 | Retrieval Ref. No. | An 12 | M | M | Same as the original transaction |
| 38 | Auth. Id. Response | an 6 | M | | Same as the original transaction |

chunks.content:
### Closures

It is the process by which the POS terminal performs an end-of-day check against the host to proceed with the debit to the th and payment to the affiliated merchant.

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Closures

chunks.content:
#### Successful balance

This process is performed when the closing process is executed and the number of transactions between the host and the POS terminal add up and the host approves the closure.

**Format definition**

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | N 4 | 0500 | 0510 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 3 | Processing Code | n 6 | 920000 | 920000 | |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | | M | hhmmss |
| 13 | Date, Local Trans. | n 4 | | M | MMDD |
| 24 | NII | N 3 | M | M | |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | | M | |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 60 | Batch Number | Ans... 999 | M | Or | Open lot number |
| 63 | Total / Resp Text | Ans... 999 | M | M | See Closing Totals |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Closures > Successful balance

chunks.content:
#### Batch Upload

The batch upload occurs when there are differences in the totals in the closure, the host responds to the 510 with a response code "95" which tells the terminal to proceed with the batch upload.

**Format definition**

| Bit | Data Element Name | Attribute | Request | Response | Comments |
|-----|-------------------|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
| | Message Type Id | N 4 | 0320 | 0330 | |
| | Bit Map | b 64 | M | M | |
| 2 | Primary Acct. Num. | n .. 19 | C06 | | |
| 3 | Processing Code | N 6 | M | M | All transactions must have the same code as the original transaction but replacing the last digit with 1. Ex(Orig : 000000 → 320 : 000001). This is for all transactions except for the last one that maintains the same code, indicating to the Host that it has already been uploaded all transactions |
| 4 | Amount, Trans. | N 12 | M | Or | Amount of the original transaction |
| 11 | Systems Trace No | n 6 | M | M | The consecutive must follow |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | M | M | Time in approval. Hhmmss |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Closures > Batch Upload

chunks.content:
The consecutive must follow |
| 12 | Time, Local Trans. | n 6 | M | M | Time in approval. Hhmmss |

| 13 | Date, Local Trans. | n 4 | M | M | Date on approval. MMDD |
| 14 | Date, Expiration | n 4 | C06 | | YYMM |
| 22 | POS Entry Mode | n 3 | M | | |
| 24 | NII | N 3 | M | | Same as the original transaction |

| 38 | Auth. Id. Response | an 6 | M | | Same as the original transaction |
| 39 | Response Code | an 2 | M | M | Same as the original transaction |
| 41 | Terminal Id | Ans 8 | M | M | |
| 42 | Card Acq. Id | Ans 15 | M | | |
| 54 | Additional Amounts | an... 120 | M | | If the original transaction carries tax and gratuity, they must also be sent in the 320 |
| 60 | Original Data | Ans... 999 | M | | Original transaction data: Request MessageType + Request System Trace Number + "000000000000" Ex: "0200123456000000000000" |

metadata.heading_path: ISO-8583 Message Specification > DEFINING THE TRANSACTION FORMAT > Closures > Batch Upload

**NOTES:**

Sensitive data such as tracks and bit55 should never travel in batch upload messages. At the end of the batch upload (320 messages) the terminal must send message 500 with a process code '960000'
